summary.hostile_finding: The atlas mostly maps tensions, uncertainties, benefit-risk tradeoffs, and classification problems. It overuses 'contradiction' for cases where both propositions can be simultaneously true.

contradictions.title: Amyloid/tau pathology is central, but target removal produces modest, trial-dependent clinical benefit
contradictions.hostile_assessment: This is not a direct contradiction unless the prior claim is 'amyloid/tau removal is sufficient for large clinical benefit.' The current wording already concedes central-but-insufficient biology, so the tension is real but softened into a non-exclusive claim.

contradictions.title: Anti-amyloid benefit conflicts with vascular-amyloid, CAA, microbleed, and ARIA safety boundaries
contradictions.hostile_assessment: This is a valid benefit-risk tension, but benefit and safety risk can both be true. It is clinically important, yet it is not a mechanistic contradiction in Alzheimer etiology.

contradictions.title: Biomarker-defined Alzheimer biology conflicts with clinically heterogeneous and mixed-pathology dementia
contradictions.hostile_assessment: This is mainly a classification and generalizability problem. Biomarker-defined biology and mixed clinical pathology are compatible; the contradiction exists only if biomarker positivity is overclaimed as the dominant clinical cause.

contradictions.title: Tau tracks stage and symptoms, yet tau-directed therapies have not settled causality
contradictions.hostile_assessment: Correlation with symptoms and incomplete therapeutic validation are not contradictory. This is an unresolved causal and translational question.

contradictions.title: Microglia and immune activation appear both protective and damaging
contradictions.hostile_assessment: The opposing roles can coexist by disease stage, cell state, and context. It is a real biological tension, but not a strict contradiction.

contradictions.title: Vascular pathology is treated as risk factor, comorbidity, and co-causal dementia mechanism
contradictions.hostile_assessment: These categories are not mutually exclusive. A factor can be a risk factor, comorbidity, and contributor. The issue is attribution and weighting.

contradictions.title: Mitochondrial/metabolic dysfunction could be upstream driver, downstream consequence, or parallel aging process
contradictions.hostile_assessment: The title explicitly states alternative causal orderings. That is uncertainty, not contradiction.

contradictions.title: APOE evidence supports amyloid biology but also lipid, immune, glial, and vascular pathways
contradictions.hostile_assessment: Pleiotropy is expected for APOE and does not contradict amyloid involvement. This is pathway broadening, not contradiction.

contradictions.title: Blood biomarkers show strong diagnostic promise but contested deployment thresholds and generalizability
contradictions.hostile_assessment: Diagnostic promise and implementation uncertainty are compatible. This is an implementation/threshold uncertainty, not a contradiction.

contradictions.title: LATE, PART, vascular cognitive impairment, and other age-related pathologies can mimic or combine with AD
contradictions.hostile_assessment: Mimicry and copathology complicate classification. They do not contradict AD biology unless the atlas assumes pure single-pathology clinical AD.

contradictions.title: Single-target disease-modifying trials conflict with multi-factorial and stage-dependent disease models
contradictions.hostile_assessment: This is a trial-design tension. Single-target trials can be valid tests inside a multifactorial model, so the conflict is partial and overgeneralized.

contradictions.title: Symptom-network and synaptic failure evidence conflicts with pathology-only endpoint confidence
contradictions.hostile_assessment: Clinical symptoms, synaptic loss, and pathology endpoints are different levels of measurement. The atlas does not show an actual contradiction, only a warning against endpoint overconfidence.
